results.rendered_html:
ut-√sah (ud-√sah) Ā. -sahate (inf. -saham,  TBr. i, 1, 6, 1 ) to endure, bear,  TBr. ;  AitBr. iii, 44, 5 ;  ŚBr. i, 3, 3, 13 ;
to be able, be adequate, have power (with inf. or dat. of abstr. noun);
to act with courage or energy,  MBh. ;  R. ;  Śak. ;  Pañcat.  &c.:
Caus. -sāhayati, to animate, encourage, excite,  MBh. ;  Kathās.  :
Desid. of the Caus. (p. -sisāhayiṣat) to wish to excite or encourage,  Bhaṭṭ. ix, 69.

results.rendered_html:
utsah 1 A.
1 To be able, have power or energy; (expressed by ‘can’); dare, venture (with inf.); tavānuvṛttiṃ na ca kartumutsahe  Ku. 5. 65 ;  Ś. 5 ;  Mu. 4. 14 ;  Śi. 14. 83 ;  Bk. 3. 54 , 5. 59 , 14. 89 ; sometimes with acc. and dat. also;  Pt. 1. 
2 To attempt, be prompted or incited;  Ki. 1. 36 ; to cheer up, not to sink or give way; ajñavannotsahethāstvaṃ
(pb)  Bk. 19. 16. 
3 To feel at ease, enjoy pleasure; kṣaṇamapyutsahate na māṃ vinā  Ku. 4. 36. 
4 To go forward, march on;  K. 249. 
— Caus. To encourage, excite, instigate, incite; varuṇotsāhitena vedhasā  K. 22 ;  Bk. 9. 69.
